title: minuartia attica subsp. idaea
intro: Minuartia attica subsp. idaea, also known as Idaea or the Idæan minuartia, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae, native to Greece. It is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional folk medicine, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The plant is said to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating digestive issues, and providing relief from respiratory problems. The herbal preparation of Minuartia attica subsp. idaea typically involves drying the plant's leaves and flowers, which can then be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
